mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market slack that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in over 60% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicate supply chain bottlenecks that force immediate equity repricing, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during post-shock periods, with a hit rate below 45% or a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 235.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Markets absorb CPIAUCSL readings above 235.0 without negative 20-day returns, showing a positive return frequency exceeding 50% in triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strong residential investment and construction sector activity, which immediately lifts market sentiment and generates positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with a predictive accuracy below 48% or a Rank IC consistently negative over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding above 105.0 during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity utilization nearing limits, prompting inflation fears that drive negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above 105.0 in late-cycle regimes fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive returns occurring in over 60% of triggered instances.
